title: Scalable Preference Evaluation
concept: Human evaluation is slow and expensive; using a larger frontier model (like GPT-4) to evaluate outputs acts as a fast, scalable alternative.

exercises.prompt: What is the main drawback of relying only on humans to grade AI outputs?
exercises.explain: Human evaluation is the traditional gold standard, but it is slow and expensive. We need automated ways to evaluate our models.

cards.title: Who Judges the AI?
cards.body: If you build a new AI model, how do you know it's actually smarter than the old one? Asking humans to read and grade thousands of AI answers takes weeks and costs a fortune. What if we could use a super-smart AI to do the grading for us instantly?

cards.title: What is Scalable Evaluation?
cards.body: Scalable Preference Evaluation uses a powerful AI model (like a frontier LLM) as a judge to compare different AI outputs. Instead of waiting for humans, the judge model quickly rates which response is better, safer, or more helpful.

cards.title: The Smart Classmate
cards.body: Imagine you write stories and need feedback. Asking your busy teacher (a human evaluator) takes days. Instead, you ask the smartest student in the class (a frontier AI model) who has read thousands of books. They can instantly read all your stories and tell you which ones are best!

cards.title: AI Judging Recipes
cards.body: Imagine a chatbot created to write recipes. To test it, we generate 1,000 recipes. Instead of paying chefs to read them all, we send the recipes to a frontier model. The frontier model quickly flags which recipes are confusing or have missing steps, giving us feedback in minutes.

cards.title: Myth: AI Can't Judge Quality
cards.body: Many believe that only humans can judge if a text sounds natural or helpful. However, studies show that advanced frontier models agree with human judges most of the time, making them incredibly reliable for everyday testing.

cards.title: Speed & Cost Comparison
cards.body: Compare how we scale up testing using humans versus using a frontier AI model.

right.text: Takes minutes, costs pennies, and works 24/7.

left.text: Takes weeks, costs a lot of money, and judges get tired.

exercises.prompt: Why don't we rely solely on human judges to test AI updates?

options.text: It is too fast for developers to keep up with.
Computers refuse to read human writing.
It is very slow, expensive, and hard to scale.

exercises.explain: Human evaluation is very slow, expensive, and difficult to scale up when you have thousands of outputs to check.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Because AI evaluation is so fast, we should completely eliminate human review from our systems forever.
exercises.explain: While frontier models are excellent for fast grading, they can have biases. Combining them with human spot-checks is the best practice.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe this fast evaluation method.
exercises.sentence: Using a frontier model to evaluate outputs is a highly [blank] alternative to human grading.
exercises.explain: Using a powerful AI to judge output is highly scalable because it can grade thousands of responses in minutes, whereas humans would take weeks.

exercises.prompt: Which of these prompts is designed for an AI acting as an evaluator?

options.text: Write a poem about a lonely robot.
Read these two helper responses and decide which one is more polite and accurate.

exercises.explain: The prompt asks the judge to evaluate the quality of the response, which is a classic setup for an AI evaluator. The others are just standard chatbot tasks.

exercises.prompt: Your team has just updated your helper chatbot. You need to quickly evaluate if the new version produces better answers than the old version. What is the most scalable way to do this?

options.text: It requires hiring hundreds of human reviewers to read every single chatbot response.
We use a larger, smarter model (like GPT-4) to grade and compare the outputs of our models.
We stop evaluating our models completely and hope the users don't notice errors.
We ask a tiny, fast model to generate random grades without reading the text.

exercises.explain: Using a powerful frontier model to judge other models is called LLM-as-a-Judge. It provides a fast, consistent, and highly scalable way to evaluate text, making it much easier to test system updates without waiting for slow human reviews.
